title: Reusable Validation Runner -- PLAN ONLY (no code, no backtest, no strategy search)

provenance.head_note: HEAD 4e21cc4 is a JARVIS-only background-automation commit (app.py, docs/jarvis_system_map.json, templates/jarvis.html, tests/test_jarvis_route.py); it touched NO agentic_factory file and 18797fb remains its ancestor.

1_executive_summary: The validation ladder was built and run MANUALLY, prompt-by-prompt, across four strategy branches.
It correctly PARKED all four: Donchian (reference baseline only), S26 (PARK_CANDIDATE), S27 (IS_FAIL), S28 (IS_FAIL).
The highest-value next improvement is automation/repeatability of that ladder -- NOT immediately testing another strategy.
This step designs a reusable validation runner so any future frozen strategy can be put through the same controlled, anti-overfit ladder with consistent reports and identical git/data guardrails.

2_problem_being_solved.summary: Manual prompt-by-prompt validation is slow and error-prone.
pain_points: Repeated report patterns hand-authored each step (provenance, data QA, metrics tables, verdict).
Repeated git guardrails restated each commit (explicit pathspec, verify-before/after).
Repeated IS/OOS separation logic re-implemented per branch.
Risk of accidental OOS peeking (the 2023-2025 seal is enforced only by hand-written asserts).
Risk of mixed commits due to background JARVIS index races.
Hard to compare candidates consistently when each report is bespoke.

3_design_goal: A reusable validation runner that takes a FROZEN strategy engine + frozen spec + a branch ID/commit, and generates the SAME decision-record ladder (one module at a time, each gated) in a controlled, reproducible, anti-overfit way -- producing uniform report.json/report.md per module.
4_non_goals: NOT an agent that mutates strategies until profitable.
NOT broker-connected; no network, no data fetch.
NOT allowed to change strategy parameters.
NOT allowed to touch OOS data until the OOS protocol is committed.
NOT allowed to auto-promote a candidate.
NOT allowed to pick the best variant or hide failed branches.

A_spec_check.purpose: Verify the frozen spec exists, parameters are locked, and branch ID + selected-hypothesis commit hash are recorded.
blocks: any later module if spec missing or params not frozen

B_is_baseline.purpose: Run IS only; enforce the IS data window; hard-block OOS files; produce the IS decision record.

C_oos_protocol.purpose: Create the pass/watch/fail protocol BEFORE any OOS run; require the protocol be committed before OOS is permitted.
C_oos_protocol.gate: oos_run is refused unless a committed protocol hash is supplied

D_oos_run.purpose: Run OOS exactly ONCE; compare strictly to the committed protocol; no re-runs with changed rules.
hard_rules: protocol hash must match a committed protocol

E_entry_significance.purpose: Real entries vs same-count random-in-regime entries; optional real entries vs raw-signal baseline.
outputs: EDGE_LIKELY / INCONCLUSIVE / NOT_SUPPORTED at fixed horizons

F_sequence_risk.purpose: Trade-order Monte Carlo + bootstrap + top-winner dependence (the S25 lesson).

G_regime_breakdown.purpose: Volatility/trend/year regime breakdown + concentration analysis (single-year / single-regime dominance).

H_multimarket.purpose: NQ primary, ES independent confirmation, micros (MNQ/MES) proxy-only; no false robustness claims.
hard_rules: micros are descriptive only, never independent
max verdict by design = PARTIAL_SUPPORT when independence is by-ticker not by-underlying

I_walk_forward.purpose: Rolling 3-year and 5-year windows; identify common weak windows (e.g. the 2014-2016 chop).

J_friction_stress.purpose: Low/moderate/high/severe per-trade R-cost; compute break-even friction.
outputs: R at each friction level (IS and OOS)

K_readiness_gate.purpose: Map cumulative evidence to a readiness tier.

6_data_safety_rules: Hard-block OOS files during any IS module (refuse the run, do not silently drop).
No network fetch anywhere inside the validation runner.
Data inventory must be explicit (every input file path recorded).
The exact input file list and data window must be recorded in EVERY module report.
7_git_safety_rules: Never plain `git commit` when JARVIS may stage files.
Report the exact staged set (`git diff --cached --name-only`) BEFORE commit.
Raw artifacts (e.g. _run.json/_run.py) are NOT committed unless explicitly approved.
When HEAD advances externally, confirm the prior approved commit is still an ancestor and the external commit touched no agentic_factory file before proceeding; never touch/unstage JARVIS files.

9_runner_architecture_proposal.note: PLAN ONLY -- suggested files; NONE are created in this step.

examples: python -m engine.validation_runner --branch S29 --module is_baseline --strategy engine/s29_x.py --market NQ --window IS

11_overfitting_guardrails: The runner must NOT choose the best variant.
The runner must NOT mutate parameters.
The runner must NOT rerun OOS with changed rules.
The runner must NOT hide failed branches.
The runner must NOT combine OOS with IS to justify promotion.
The runner must NOT promote based on a single metric.

12_lessons_from_parked_branches.donchian: Profit can HIDE a weak entry and sequence fragility. S24 found ENTRY_EDGE_NOT_SUPPORTED; S25 found prob(total R<=0)=20.9%, net flips negative removing top-3, best trade 50-65% of net, realized DD only at the 11th percentile of shuffles (path luck). A profitable equity curve is not an edge.
12_lessons_from_parked_branches.s26: OOS PASS is NOT enough. NQ+ES OOS both passed and were friction-robust, but the thin ~0.06R IS edge was FRICTION_SENSITIVE, entry significance was INCONCLUSIVE, regime was INCONCLUSIVE (profitable vol regime inconsistent across markets), the record lived entirely in the post-2016 trend, and OOS was single-year-dominated (NQ 2024 61.7%, ES 2025 72.8%). Result: PARK_CANDIDATE.
12_lessons_from_parked_branches.s27_s28: IS_FAIL must STOP before OOS. Both failed IS net-negative with the anti-Donchian top-3 gate failing (net already negative; removing winners makes it worse). Burning the one-shot OOS on an IS-failed branch wastes the clean look and invites curve-fitting.
12_lessons_from_parked_branches.why_ladder_valuable: The ladder SAVES the OOS seal and prevents emotional continuation -- it converts 'this looks promising' into mechanical pass/watch/fail against pre-registered floors.

13_proposed_implementation_sequence.Factory-D2: validation report schema + report writer ONLY
13_proposed_implementation_sequence.Factory-D3: IS runner harness for one EXISTING (frozen, parked) engine
13_proposed_implementation_sequence.note: No paper/live module until much later, and only under separate explicit authorization.

14_final_recommendation.rationale: Repeatability should come before more strategy search. Four branches already proved the ladder works; encoding the report schema + writer first (Factory-D2) is the smallest, lowest-risk increment that removes the most manual error (bespoke reports, restated guardrails) and makes every later module consistent. Returning to S29 idea-sourcing now would re-incur the manual cost on every step.
14_final_recommendation.alternative: S29-D1 deeper idea-sourcing memo (no code) -- valid if the operator prefers to keep searching; but recommended to defer until the runner exists.

15_final_line: Factory-D1 is a plan-only step. No strategy test, optimization, paper trading, live trading, or runner implementation is authorized.
trading_recommendation: NONE. Plan-only design step. Donchian, S26, S27, S28 remain PARKED; no new strategy started; the runner is not built.
